title: Add social share buttons (Twitter/LinkedIn/Copy Link) to all content pages
description: No social sharing buttons exist on any content page despite excellent OG metadata. Add share buttons to: template canvas (share current design), concept pages, problem pages, blog pages. Buttons: 'Copy Link' (copies URL with state), 'Share on Twitter' (pre-filled tweet with OG link), 'Share on LinkedIn' (pre-filled post). Use existing to-url.ts for URL state encoding. Position: top-right of canvas toolbar and bottom of content pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Share buttons visible on canvas toolbar and content pages
Copy Link copies URL with canvas state (uses to-url.ts compression)
Twitter button opens tweet composer with template name + link
LinkedIn button opens share dialog with OG preview
Share buttons are keyboard accessible

title: Add teaches/assesses/timeRequired to JSON-LD LearningResource schema
description: The existing generateLearningResourceJsonLd() in json-ld.ts is missing key educational schema properties that Google uses for Learning carousel placement. Add: teaches (competency taught), assesses (competency assessed), timeRequired (ISO 8601 duration), interactivityType ('active' for visualizations), educationalUse ('self-study'). Also add dual typing @type: ['LearningResource', 'InteractiveResource'] for visualization pages.
acceptanceCriteria: LearningResource JSON-LD includes teaches, assesses, timeRequired, interactivityType
Values are concept-specific (not generic across all pages)
Schema validates against schema.org validator
All concept pages pass updated JSON-LD

title: Add bookmark/favorite feature for templates and concepts
description: No way to save/favorite templates for later review. Add a bookmark toggle (heart/star icon) on: template gallery cards, loaded template canvas header, concept pages. Store bookmarks in localStorage (via progress-store). Show 'My Bookmarks' section in dashboard and template gallery filter. Bookmarked items get priority in SRS review scheduling.
acceptanceCriteria: Bookmark toggle on template cards and loaded template header
Bookmarks persisted in localStorage via progress-store
'My Bookmarks' filter in template gallery
Bookmarked items show in dashboard 'Saved' section
Bookmarked concepts get 1.5x priority in SRS scheduling

title: Add streak freeze system (1 free freeze per week, purchasable with XP)
description: Based on Duolingo data: streak freeze reduced churn by 21%. Current streak system in streak-protector.ts resets to 0 on any miss. Add: (1) 1 free Streak Freeze per week (auto-granted Monday). (2) Additional freezes purchasable with XP (500 XP per freeze, max 3 stored). (3) Auto-apply: if user misses a day AND has a freeze, streak continues with a 'freeze used' indicator. (4) Streak Repair: within 3 hours of midnight, offer earn-back (complete one micro-challenge to save streak). Store freeze count in progress-store.
acceptanceCriteria: 1 free freeze auto-granted per week
Additional freezes purchasable with XP from progress store
Freeze auto-applies on missed day (no manual action needed)
StreakBadge shows freeze-used indicator (snowflake icon) for frozen days
3-hour repair window after midnight with micro-challenge earn-back
Max 3 freezes stored at any time

title: Add concept-specific OG preview images showing visualization screenshots
description: Current OG images (api/og/route.tsx) generate generic branded cards with text. For 40-60% higher CTR on social sharing, generate concept-specific preview images showing a mini-screenshot of the actual visualization (e.g., a consistent hashing ring, an architecture diagram with particles flowing). Update the OG route to accept concept-specific render requests and generate visual previews.
acceptanceCriteria: OG images for concept pages show a visual preview of the concept (not just text)
Template OG images show the architecture diagram thumbnail
Preview renders within 5 seconds (Edge runtime constraint)
Fallback to branded text card if preview generation fails

title: Add weekly learning recap email with 3 behavioral segments
description: The existing weeklyDigestEmail() in templates.ts has 3 basic stats in a table. Upgrade to Duolingo-style recap with: (1) Week-over-week comparison with up/down arrows, (2) Visual bar graph of daily activity, (3) Concept mastery progress bar, (4) 3 behavioral variants: Active (celebrate), At-risk (streak urgency), Dormant (knowledge decay urgency). Store prior week stats for comparison. Add 'fading concepts' section showing SRS concepts below 85% retention.
acceptanceCriteria: Weekly email includes: XP summary, streak count, challenges completed, concept progress
Week-over-week delta with up/down indicators
3 template variants: Active (celebration), At-risk (urgency), Dormant (re-engagement)
'Knowledge fading' section for concepts below 85% retention
Single CTA button linking to most impactful next action

title: Add embeddable widget endpoint for external sites
description: No way to embed Architex visualizations in external websites (blogs, documentation, course platforms). Create /embed/[concept-slug] route that renders a lightweight, standalone version of any visualization (no chrome, no navigation, minimal JS). Add 'Embed' tab to export dialog that generates iframe code. Every embed includes 'Powered by Architex' attribution link. Existing oEmbed provider (api/oembed/route.ts) handles auto-expansion in Slack/Notion. This adds the manual embed path.
acceptanceCriteria: /embed/[concept-slug] renders standalone visualization
Export dialog has 'Embed' tab with copyable iframe code
Embed includes responsive wrapper suggestion
Embed includes 'Powered by Architex' attribution link
Embed works cross-origin with sandbox='allow-scripts' iframe

title: Add content creation CLI tool for template scaffolding
description: Adding a new template currently requires manually creating a JSON file and referencing 55 existing templates for the correct schema. Create a CLI tool: `pnpm content:new [template-name]` that: (1) Prompts for template metadata (name, difficulty, category, description), (2) Scaffolds a complete JSON file with all required fields pre-filled with placeholders, (3) Validates the JSON against the DiagramTemplate schema, (4) Generates SEO metadata stubs (OG, JSON-LD), (5) Runs `pnpm content:validate` to check all templates for schema compliance. This reduces new template creation from hours to minutes.
acceptanceCriteria: `pnpm content:new` creates a valid template JSON with all required fields
`pnpm content:validate` checks all templates against DiagramTemplate schema
CLI output includes next steps (which fields to fill in)
Generated template passes schema validation immediately
Works for both system-design templates and other module types

title: Add Knowledge Decay dashboard widget showing fading concepts
description: The FSRS system tracks retrievability (R) per concept but this data isn't surfaced to users. Add a 'Fading Concepts' widget to the dashboard showing: concepts whose R has dropped below 85%, sorted by urgency. Each concept shows: name, current R%, days since last review, 'Review Now' button. Leverages the forgetting curve to create urgency: 'Consistent Hashing dropped to 72% retention — review in 2 minutes before it fades further.' This creates a reason to return daily.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard shows 'Fading Concepts' section with concepts below 85% R
Each concept shows: name, current retention %, days since review
'Review Now' button links directly to SRS session for that concept
Sorted by urgency (lowest R first)
Empty state: 'All concepts strong! Come back tomorrow to check.'

title: Add personalized 'Next Up' recommendation based on skill gaps
description: Current learning paths (learning-paths.ts) are static sequences. Add dynamic 'Next Up' recommendation that considers: (1) Completed challenges, (2) SRS retention levels per concept, (3) Skill assessment gaps, (4) Time since last review per topic. Algorithm: find the concept with highest (importance × (1 - mastery_level)) score. Show on dashboard as a prominent 'Continue Learning' card with the recommended template and why it's recommended ('Your caching knowledge is strong but you haven't practiced sharding — try this next').
acceptanceCriteria: 'Continue Learning' card on dashboard with personalized next template
Recommendation explains WHY this template was chosen ('Your X is weak because Y')
One-click to start the recommended template
Updates after each completed challenge or SRS review
Falls back to difficulty-based progression if no skill data exists

title: Write comprehensive Template Author's Guide with examples and checklist
description: CONTRIBUTING.md has 10 lines on template creation. Write a detailed guide at docs/guides/template-authors-guide.md covering: (1) Template JSON schema reference (every field explained), (2) Step-by-step creation walkthrough, (3) 3 annotated examples (easy, medium, hard templates), (4) LearnSteps writing guide (how to write educational steps), (5) Simulation config guide (chaos scenarios, cost breakdown), (6) Quality checklist (minimum requirements before submission), (7) Common mistakes to avoid. This enables non-engineers (CS professors, content creators) to contribute templates.
acceptanceCriteria: Guide covers all DiagramTemplate schema fields with examples
3 annotated template examples (easy, medium, hard) included
LearnSteps writing guide with good/bad examples
Quality checklist with minimum requirements
A CS professor could follow the guide to create a template without engineering help

title: Add template JSON schema validator to CI pipeline
description: No automated validation for template JSON files. Invalid templates can be committed without detection. Add: (1) JSON schema validator that checks all files in templates/system-design/ against DiagramTemplate type, (2) Checks: required fields present, difficulty in range 1-5, node types match nodeTypes registry, edge types match edgeTypes registry, simulation chaos node refs exist in template. (3) Run as pre-commit hook and in CI. (4) Report clear error messages for each violation.
acceptanceCriteria: Validator runs on all template JSON files in CI
Catches: missing required fields, invalid difficulty range, orphan node references
Validates simulation chaos node IDs exist in template
Clear error messages: 'template.json:simulation.chaos[0].targetNodes[0] references node "dedup-service" which does not exist in template'
Pre-commit hook prevents invalid templates from being committed
